title: Alone
content:
A very old woman
 Lives in yon house - 
 The squeak of the cricket,
 The stir of the mouse,
 Are all she knows
 Of the earth and us.

 Once she was young,
 Would dance and play,
 Like many another
 Young popinjay;
 And run to her mother
 At dusk of day.

 And colours bright
 She delighted in;
 The fiddle to hear,
 And to lift her chin,
 And sing as small
 As a twittering wren.

 But age apace
 Comes at last to all;
 And a lone house filled
 With the cricket's call;
 And the scampering mouse
 In the hollow wall.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Walter De La Mare delivers a powerful performance in "Alone"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
